title: Ensure rootless containers without a passwd can start
body:
We want to modify /etc/passwd to add an entry for the user in question, but at the same time we don't want to require the container provide a /etc/passwd (a container with a single, statically linked binary and nothing else is perfectly fine and should be allowed, for example). We could create the passwd file if it does not exist, but if the container doesn't provide one,it's probably better not to make one at all. Gate changes to /etc/passwd behind a stat() of the file in the container returning cleanly.

Fixes #7515

Want to backport this to 2.0 for 2.0.6
Will look into adding a test - probably can build an image based on alpine that rm's /etc/passwd

LGTM, but what do you feel is the desired behavior of:
```
$ printf "FROM alpine\nRUN rm -f /etc/passwd /etc/shadow /etc/group /etc/gshadow\n" | ./bin/podman build -t foo -
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN rm -f /etc/passwd /etc/shadow /etc/group /etc/gshadow
STEP 3: COMMIT foo
--> 79cc4e80cf8
79cc4e80cf8014bac884645dda7be3ddba9b3d8fddd166f1cd52085341cbe2e5
 
$ ./bin/podman run --rm --userns=keep-id foo cat /etc/passwd
cat: can't open '/etc/passwd': No such file or directory
```
This should not be a failure.  I think if the container image does not have a /etc/passwd or /etc/group then we should NOT create one.
@edsantiago IMO, that should fail - if the image does not have /etc/passwd we should never create it.

3 separate network errors, wow.

Retrying all, things will hopefully go green now. @rhatdan @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 @ashley-cui PTAL

Tests green. Release hold if you wish, or leave it if you'd like further review.

/lgtm
/hold
